description: The call happened, and near the end the applicant asked not to be contacted again outside of anything already agreed. FLAG (mirrors post_call_synthesis.consent_revoked, issue #68 precedent): no canonical consent entity/gate exists yet, and consent/cooldown enforcement is scoped to the agents that actually INITIATE new contact, not this one. This agent classifies objections only — it should still succeed, but the summary must not recommend further outreach to 'resolve' any objection beyond what was already agreed, and no objection should be mischaracterized as something requiring an unsolicited follow-up.

interaction.notes: Call happened as scheduled. Morgan raised a price objection early on. Near the end, Morgan asked not to be contacted again outside of anything already agreed and said they'd reach out themselves if interested later.

evidenceRecords.content: Call happened as scheduled. Morgan raised a price objection early on. Near the end, Morgan asked not to be contacted again outside of anything already agreed and said they'd reach out themselves if interested later.

output_schema_constraints: the price objection is captured as classification 'observed' with sourceRef 'interaction.note.primary'
summary does not recommend initiating further outreach to resolve the price objection — at most it notes the applicant will reach out themselves
the revoked/limited-contact consent signal is not itself fabricated into an 'objection' entry — it is a separate signal this agent is not responsible for enforcing (FLAG, see description)
